2606.09692.title: Observability for Delegated Execution in Agentic AI Systems
2606.09692.paper_summary: Delegation-scoped execution is not identifiable from standard observables: audit logs and execution traces can be identical under multiple incompatible delegation assignments. This gap is especially acute in LLM-based agentic systems, where agents dynamically select tools, vary execution sequences across runs for the same instruction, and spawn cooperating sub-agents. These dynamics fragment and interleave traces, making delegation-scoped reconstruction from causal structure alone structurally underdetermined. Although individual actions are authorized and logged, existing audit, tracing, and security schemas lack the semantics to reconstruct what actions occurred under a given delegation across heterogeneous systems. We focus on delegation-scoped attribution and access/share footprint reconstruction, not intent inference or reasoning reconstruction. We present an agent-aware observability substrate consisting of a lightweight gateway and a common information model that binds delegation context at execution time. This enables reliable cross-tool delegation-scoped reconstruction and direct forensic queries without heuristic time-window correlation.
2606.09692.paper_summary_zh: 委派範疇的執行無法從標準可觀察項中識別：審計日誌和執行痕跡在多個不相容的委派分配下可能是相同的。這一差距在基於大型語言模型的代理系統中尤為明顯，這些系統中的代理會動態選擇工具，對於相同的指令在不同執行中變化執行序列，並產生協作的子代理。這些動態使得痕跡碎片化並交錯，使得僅從因果結構進行委派範疇的重建在結構上無法確定。儘管個別行動是經過授權並被記錄的，但現有的審計、追蹤和安全架構缺乏重建在異質系統下給定委派下發生了哪些行動的語義。我們專注於委派範疇的歸因和訪問/共享足跡重建，而非意圖推斷或推理重建。我們提出了一個代理感知的可觀察性基礎設施，由一個輕量級的網關和一個在執行時綁定委派上下文的通用信息模型組成。這使得可靠的跨工具委派範疇重建和直接的法醫查詢成為可能，而無需啟發式的時間窗口關聯。
2606.09692.author: Abhinav Mishra et.al.
